channel.title: morningNewsSection1
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from morningNewsSection1, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Inside a secretive Ukrainian team launching deep drone strikes at Russia
item.description: Ukraine's long-range drones strike deep inside Russia, up to 1,200 miles away, hitting oil refineries. NPR spent time with the team.

item.title: If a Lyme disease vaccine is approved, will people take it? We asked hunters
item.description: Drugmakers are developing a new shot to prevent tick-borne illness. How might it fare in an era of vaccine skepticism?

item.title: Mauricio Pochettino turned the U.S. World Cup team into believers
item.description: Pochettino was the biggest hire in U.S. men's soccer history. His rebuild had rough patches, but now the team is World Cup-bound.

item.title: Supreme Court expands Trump's immigration powers, raising concerns over population decline
item.description: The U.S. population, already aging and declining, faces further drops after the Supreme Court upheld Trump's authority to deport hundreds of thousands.
